items.shape_blurb: A back four with a single pivot behind two eights, and a striker flanked by two wide forwards.
strengths_json: Natural triangles everywhere, built for wide overloads, third-man play, and a high press.
The default vehicle of positional-play identities.
vulnerabilities_json: The space beside the single pivot: a diamond or a ten drifting into the half-space attacks where the lone six cannot reach.
Counters through the space behind attacking fullbacks.

items.shape_blurb: A back four with a double pivot, and a ten flanked by two wingers behind a lone nine.
strengths_json: The most balanced modern shape: the double pivot gives counter-security and two build-up outlets.
The ten gets a protected creative station, and the shape converts cleanly to a 4-4-2 mid-block without the ball.
vulnerabilities_json: The sides of the double pivot, in the half-spaces in front of the fullbacks.
A lone nine can get isolated if the ten and wingers do not support quickly.

items.name: 4-4-2 (flat)
items.shape_blurb: Two banks of four, and two strikers permanently occupying both centre backs.
strengths_json: Simplest to teach, and the gold standard defensive shape: compact, with clear zones and jobs.
Two strikers permanently occupy both centre backs, ideal for counters and second balls.
vulnerabilities_json: Outnumbered two against three in central midfield versus any three-man midfield.
The space between the lines behind the midfield four, Zone 14, if the banks stretch apart.

items.shape_blurb: Three centre backs, two wingbacks owning the flanks, three central midfielders, and two strikers.
strengths_json: Central overload of three midfielders plus two strikers, plus a spare centre back in build-up.
Wingbacks give width without sacrificing back-line numbers.
vulnerabilities_json: The space behind the wingbacks is the formation's known weakness.
Wide centre backs get dragged into unfamiliar one-against-ones in the channels.

items.shape_blurb: Three centre backs, two wingbacks, two central midfielders, and a front three.
strengths_json: Ferocious in the press: the front three plus wingbacks jumping means five pressers.
Wide overloads from wingback and wide forward pairs, plus a spare man in build-up.
vulnerabilities_json: Only two central midfielders, so a three-man midfield can overrun them.

items.shape_blurb: A back five, a midfield four or three, and a lone striker or two, the low-block reference shape.
strengths_json: The park-the-bus reference shape: covers the full width of the box with five defenders.
Kills crosses and cutbacks, funnelling everything into low-value long shots.
vulnerabilities_json: Almost no possession relief: without a functioning outlet the block faces ninety minutes of siege.
Second balls around the box become constant emergencies.
